ud2.2:1.2: on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Sāvatthī at Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
ud2.2:1.3:
And on that occasion a large number of monks, after the meal, on returning from their alms round,
were sitting gathered together in the assembly hall when this discussion arose:
ud2.2:1.4:
“Friends, which of these two kings has greater wealth, greater possessions, the greater treasury,
the larger realm, the greater stock of riding animals, the greater army, greater power, greater might:
King Seniya Bimbisāra of Magadha or King Pasenadi of Kosala?”
ud2.2:1.5: And this discussion came to no conclusion.
ud2.2:2.1:
Then the Blessed One, emerging from his seclusion in the late afternoon,
went to the assembly hall and, on arrival, sat down on a seat laid out.
ud2.2:2.3:
“For what topic are you sitting together here?
And what was the discussion that came to no conclusion?”
ud2.2:3.1:
“Just now, lord, after the meal, on returning from our alms round,
we were sitting gathered here at the assembly hall when this discussion arose:
ud2.2:3.2:
‘Friends, which of these two kings has greater wealth, greater possessions, the greater treasury,
the larger realm, the greater stock of riding animals, the greater army, greater power, greater might:
King Seniya Bimbisāra of Magadha or King Pasenadi of Kosala?’
ud2.2:3.3: This was the discussion that had come to no conclusion when the Blessed One arrived.”
ud2.2:4.1:
“It isn’t proper, monks, that sons of good families,
on having gone forth out of faith from home to the homeless life, should talk on such a topic.
ud2.2:4.2: When you have gathered you have two duties:
ud2.2:5.1: Then, on realizing the significance of that, the Blessed One on that occasion exclaimed:
ud2.2:6.1: Any sensual bliss in the world,
ud2.2:6.4: of the bliss of the ending of craving.
